//: Sample configuration for the FactoryOS plugin runtime (Commit 0022). Bind 'Plugins:Runtime' into appsettings alongside the framework's own 'Plugins' section. These are PLATFORM settings, never per-customer ones: which folder packages are read from, whether a signature is mandatory, how many superseded versions are retained so a rollback has somewhere to go. Which plugins a factory runs, what it grants them and how they are configured is tenant data, held by the runtime — not settings here. NO SIGNING KEY BELONGS IN THIS FILE: keys are resolved through IPluginSigningKeySource, from the environment or a vault.

Runtime.//PlatformVersion: What packages declare their compatibility against. Configurable so an operator can hold the advertised version back for one release while third-party packages catch up.
Runtime.//RequireSignature: An INVALID signature is always fatal, whatever this says. This switch governs only the ABSENT one: leave it false for a host that runs only first-party packages compiled into the monolith; set it true for any host that installs Store packages.
Runtime.//RetainedVersions: How many superseded package versions are kept. This is what makes a rollback possible; at 0 an update is one-way.
Runtime.//AuditLifecycle: Whether successful transitions are audited. Failures are audited regardless.
Runtime.//DefaultQuota: Applied to an instance that does not narrow it further. 0 means unlimited.
